summary: Machine policy for lock screen slideshow behavior. The local Microsoft ControlPanelDisplay.admx file documents the same Personalization policy key and NoLockScreenSlideshow value used by the app.

setting.name: Lock Screen Slideshow
setting.casual_explanation: Windows can run a slideshow on the lock screen instead of always showing a single image. This policy decides whether users can use that slideshow feature.
setting.why_people_change_it: People disable it to keep the lock screen static, reduce distraction, or simplify the lock-screen experience on managed systems.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Disabling it makes the lock screen more predictable and static, but it removes slideshow personalization.
setting.professional_notes: Microsoft documents NoLockScreenSlideshow through ADMX_ControlPanelDisplay and local policy files map it to HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Personalization\NoLockScreenSlideshow. The public documentation clearly describes the enabled policy behavior and the not-configured or disabled baseline behavior, but it does not publish a raw DWORD table, so this record models the documented enforced state plus the default unset state.

allowed_values.label: Not configured
allowed_values.meaning: Users can still enable a lock-screen slideshow because the policy is not being forced.

allowed_values.label: Prevent lock-screen slideshow
allowed_values.meaning: The policy is enabled and no lock-screen slideshow will ever start.

app_current_implementation.notes: The current app writes NoLockScreenSlideshow = 1, which matches the documented enabled policy state.

windows_defaults.label: Windows machine-policy default
windows_defaults.applies_to: Windows 10 version 2004 with KB5005101 and later, plus Windows 11 systems exposing ADMX_ControlPanelDisplay

states.rationale: Microsoft documents slideshow availability when the policy is disabled or not configured.

windows_defaults.notes: The default behavior leaves lock-screen slideshow available to users.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows default
intended_for: Users who like lock-screen slideshow personalization

states.rationale: Leave lock-screen slideshow behavior under Windows control.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want slideshow personalization to remain available.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want a static lock screen only.
tradeoffs: Users can still configure slideshow behavior.
The lock screen may feel busier or less predictable.

recommended_profiles.label: Static lock screen
recommended_profiles.good_when: You want the lock screen to stay static and predictable.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want photo slideshow behavior on the lock screen.
tradeoffs: No slideshow can start on the lock screen.

evidence.title: Microsoft ADMX_ControlPanelDisplay Policy CSP
evidence.summary: Microsoft documents the lock-screen personalization policies on this page, including NoLockScreenSlideshow and the baseline behavior when the policy is disabled or not configured.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft ControlPanelDisplay.admx mapping
evidence.summary: The local ADMX file maps the machine policy to HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Personalization\NoLockScreenSlideshow.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft ControlPanelDisplay.adml help text
evidence.summary: The local ADML help text says enabling the policy prevents lock-screen slideshow settings and ensures no slide show will ever start, while slideshow remains available by default.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider writes NoLockScreenSlideshow = 1 for the current tweak.

evidence.title: Existing tweak source record
evidence.summary: The repo already tracks provenance for this tweak in the current catalog output.

decision.why: The local Microsoft ControlPanelDisplay.admx file explicitly defines NoLockScreenSlideshow under Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Personalization, and the paired ADML help text documents that enabling the policy prevents lock-screen slideshows from playing. The app writes that exact enabled state.

validation_proof.notes: The local official Microsoft ADMX file names the same machine policy surface used by the app. The paired ADML help text states that enabling the setting prevents a slide show from playing on the lock screen.
